lesson.menu_desc: First-contact e-mails and polite requests to international partners -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Writing Professional E-mails: First Contact and Information Requests
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Eduardo | Aula 15 -- Writing Professional E-mails (First Contact & Requests)

listenings.text: When a new coffee producer joins the Impact program, I write a first-contact email. First, I write a clear subject line, regarding the farm registration. Then I write the greeting: Dear, and the name of the partner. In the first line, I introduce myself. I write my name, my role, and my company. Then I write the reason for my email. I am writing to request the farm data. I always use polite requests, like Could you please send me the documents. At the end, I write a friendly closing and my signature, with my name and my role. A professional email is short, clear, and polite.

Hi Carlos, this is Emma. I read your first-contact email to the new partner in Uganda, and it is very good. The greeting is professional, and you introduce yourself clearly in the first line. The request is polite: Could you please send me the documents. I have two small tips before you send it. Regarding the subject line, make it short and clear. And in the signature, please add your phone number, not only your name and your role. Thank you for the good work, Carlos. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, the subject line: regarding your farm registration. Then the greeting: Dear Ms. Nakato, I hope this email finds you well. Next, I introduce myself: my name is Carlos Eduardo, and I work at Sucafina Brazil. Then the request: I am writing to request your farm data. Finally, the closing and signature: thank you in advance, best regards, Carlos Eduardo.
